tldr: Checkout.com and Modern Treasury serve different layers: Checkout.com is a card-acceptance PSP, Modern Treasury orchestrates US bank transfers and ledgering. A migration usually means routing bank money-movement to Modern Treasury while leaving card acceptance on Checkout.com or another PSP.
why_switch: Native ACH, wire, RTP, FedNow APIs
Better fit for high-volume bank transfers than card PSP
what_to_export: Internal ledger entries tied to Checkout.com events
export_method: Decide which flows actually move: card acceptance typically stays on Checkout.com (or another PSP), while bank money-movement (ACH, wire, RTP, FedNow) moves to Modern Treasury. Use Checkout.com's standard reporting tools to export settlement files, payout schedules, counterparty data, and reconciliation reports. Document any internal ledger entries that depend on Checkout.com webhooks so they can be repointed. Save webhook signing secrets and payload samples. Keep finance reports as the cross-cutover audit trail; they are your finance-grade source of truth.
import_method: Onboard with Modern Treasury, including bank partnership setup since they integrate with your originating bank. Configure counterparties and payment orders for the rails you actually use (ACH, wire, RTP, FedNow). Wire your ledger system to Modern Treasury's Ledgers API or use it as your real-time ledger source. Re-implement webhook handlers against their event format and signature scheme. Run test transactions per rail in sandbox, then small production amounts, before ramping. Continue running Checkout.com for card acceptance unless you are replacing that separately.

concept_mapping.target: Out of scope; keep on Checkout.com or another PSP

effort: 2-6 months depending on bank partnership and ledger integration scope
gotchas: Modern Treasury is not a card processor; card acceptance stays elsewhere
US bank focus — limited fit for global money movement
Bank partnership and onboarding can dominate the timeline
Real-time rails (RTP/FedNow) have eligibility and bank-side requirements
Webhook formats and signature verification differ and must be re-implemented
Refunds and disputes on existing Checkout.com card transactions still flow through Checkout.com

email.subject: Adding Modern Treasury for bank ops — Checkout.com stays for cards
email.body:
Hi team,

We are moving our US bank money-movement from Checkout.com to Modern Treasury. Card acceptance will stay on Checkout.com. The full program runs beyond 14 days because of bank onboarding, but here is the kickoff plan.

Days 1-3: scope which rails move (ACH, wire, RTP, FedNow) and confirm bank partnership.
Days 4-7: open Modern Treasury, configure counterparties, design ledger integration.
Days 8-11: build sandbox flows, send test transactions on each rail.
Days 12-14: finalize production rollout plan and reconciliation handoff.

Checkout.com remains our card PSP and will continue handling refunds/disputes for existing card charges.

Thanks,
Your customer
